description: Proof that Biller Experience requests discover and invoke Microsoft Foundry agents under the same distributed trace.
title: IC — Foundry agent orchestration

panels.title: Foundry invocations by agent and outcome

azureLogAnalytics.query:
dependencies
| where $__timeFilter(timestamp)
| where name == "foundry.agent.invoke"
| extend agent_id = tostring(customDimensions["gen_ai.agent.id"]), outcome = iff(success, "succeeded", "failed")
| summarize invocations = count() by agent_id, outcome, bin(timestamp, 5m)
| order by timestamp asc

dependencies
| where $__timeFilter(timestamp)
| where name == "foundry.agent.invoke"
| summarize p50_ms = percentile(duration, 50), p95_ms = percentile(duration, 95) by bin(timestamp, 5m)
| order by timestamp asc

panels.title: Recent API-to-Foundry traces
panels.description: Each row is an API-correlated Foundry call. Open operation_Id in Application Insights transaction search to inspect the request, orchestration, inventory, and agent spans together.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let apiOperations = requests
| where $__timeFilter(timestamp)
| where name has "/chat"
| project operation_Id, api_request = name;
dependencies
| where $__timeFilter(timestamp)
| where name in ("foundry.agents.list", "foundry.agent.invoke", "foundry.research.dispatch")
| join kind=inner apiOperations on operation_Id
| extend agent_id = tostring(customDimensions["gen_ai.agent.id"])
| project timestamp, operation_Id, api_request, span = name, agent_id, success, duration, resultCode
| order by timestamp desc
| take 100
